title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Sat Apr 25, 2026
summary: Somali pirates hijacked the Palau-flagged fuel tanker Honour 25 on April 25, seizing 17 multinational crew (10 Pakistanis, 4 Indonesians, and others) approximately 30 nautical miles offshore between Hafun and Bandarbeyla on the Puntland coast; the vessel was carrying 18,500 barrels of fuel from Berbera to Mogadishu. EU NAVFOR Atalanta and Combined Task Force 151 deployed in response, while no ransom demands had been issued in initial reporting. The hijacking follows an April 22 possible armed boarding off Xaafuun, confirming a sharpening piracy resurgence along Somalia's northeastern coast since October 2025. The constitutional crisis continues with President Mohamud's term expiry on May 15 now 20 days away, the Somali Future Council's Salvation Summit ultimatum active, and the SNA/Macawisley counter-offensive to retake Al-Shabaab-held Adan Yabal entering Day 6 in the stalled mobilization phase.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Fri Apr 24, 2026
summary: Somalia's opposition escalated its constitutional confrontation on April 24, threatening a parallel presidential election 'within one month' if President Mohamud refuses talks before his May 15 term expiry (21 days away); the Somali Future Council called a 'Salvation Summit.' The EU approved €75 million for AUSSOM on April 23 under the European Peace Facility — the largest single EU contribution and a critical offset to the US veto of UN cost-sharing. The SNA/Macawisley counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal (seized by Al-Shabaab on April 19) remains stalled on Day 5, with Al-Shabaab consolidating defenses and AFRICOM's Somalia air campaign tracking toward 58+ strikes for 2026. Somalia's convergent political, security, and humanitarian crises — with 6.5 million facing acute hunger — show no sign of resolution.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Thu Apr 23, 2026
summary: NISA and international partners killed 33 Al-Shabaab fighters in a two-phase precision raid on Guulane, Middle Shabelle on April 22 — one of the largest single intelligence-led operations of 2026, targeting ~90 fighters assembled to plan new attacks. AFRICOM conducted its 57th Somalia airstrike of 2026 on April 21 near Wadajir (~90km SW of Kismayo), continuing the unprecedented 200+/year pace. President Mohamud inaugurated the new Ministry of Ports and Marine Transport on April 23, promoting Somalia's 'blue economy revolution' as Turkish TPAO continues drilling the Curad-1 offshore oil well. The constitutional crisis deepens with 22 days to the presidential term expiry (May 15), no electoral framework agreed, and the SNA counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal still mobilizing.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Wed Apr 22, 2026
summary: Somalia's opposition formally declared that President Mohamud will lose all legitimacy after May 15 (23 days away), following mass defections of senior government officials — including the former NISA director — to the opposition at a meeting hosted by former President Sharif Sheikh Ahmed. EU NAVFOR Atalanta is investigating a possible armed boarding of a product tanker approximately 24 NM southeast of Xaafuun, the Horn of Africa tip, in the latest piracy incident since the resurgence began in October 2025. The SNA/Macawisley counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal (seized by Al-Shabaab on April 19) continues to mobilize from Beledweyne. Somalia's triple crisis — constitutional vacuum, active insurgency, and 6.5 million facing acute hunger with only 13.4% of the HRP funded — intensifies with no resolution in sight.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Tue Apr 21, 2026
summary: SNA forces and Macawisley clan militia are mobilizing a counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal after Al-Shabaab seized the Hiiraan Region town on April 19, killing 8 civilians including 5 women. The seizure exploits Somalia's deepening constitutional vacuum, with the presidential term now 24 days from expiry (May 15) and no electoral framework agreed. UN agencies confirmed on April 21 that 6.5 million Somalis face acute hunger — one-third of the population — with the $852M HRP only 13.4% funded and WFP warning food aid could halt entirely this month. 1.8 million children under five face acute malnutrition with 500,000 at severe risk.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Mon Apr 20, 2026
summary: Al-Shabaab seized Adan Yabal in Hiiraan Region on April 19, killing 8 civilians including 5 women, prompting President Mohamud to convene an emergency meeting with SNA Commander Gen. Yusuf Odowaa Rage and order an immediate offensive. AFRICOM announced its 56th Somalia airstrike of 2026 (AFRICOM PR #36380): a strike against ISIS-Somalia approximately 30 miles southeast of Bosaso, Puntland, on April 17. Somalia's constitutional crisis deepened with the presidential term now 25 days from expiry (May 15) and no electoral agreement in sight, as Puntland maintains its 'constitutional vacuum' declaration.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Sun Apr 19, 2026
summary: U.S. Al-Shabaab seized the strategic town of Adan Yabal (Aadan Yabaal) in Hiiraan Region in heavy clashes with Somali National Army forces around April 19, 2026.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Sat Apr 18, 2026
summary: Somalia's constitutional crisis escalated sharply: Parliament's mandate expired April 14, Speaker Madobe unilaterally extended it one year — rejected by Puntland, which formally declared a 'constitutional vacuum' on April 17 with the presidential term expiring May 15. On the military front, SNA and Jubbaland forces killed 54 Al-Shabaab fighters in Juba regions airstrikes (Apr 15-16) and 27 more in ground operations (Apr 18); NISA killed 9 in Lower Shabelle. Turkish-trained Gorgor forces were deployed around a Mogadishu military camp amid fear of military fragmentation. Diplomatically, Somalia condemned Israel's ambassador appointment to Somaliland and issued an extraordinary (though unenforceable) threat of Bab el-Mandeb restrictions; a Somali senator labeled Turkey 'resources pirates' over the TPAO offshore oil concession.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Fri Apr 17, 2026
summary: Somali forces and AUSSOM (AU Mission in Somalia) troops jointly captured a senior Al-Shabaab commander on April 17, 2026, in an operation that Hiiraan.com confirmed. Puntland's cabinet formally declared on April 17, 2026, that Somalia had 'entered constitutional ambiguity,' citing Articles 60 and 91 of Somalia's Provisional Constitution. Somali Senator Labels Turkey 'Resources Pirate'; Calls TPAO Offshore Oil Drilling Agreements Illegal and Lacking Parliament Approval.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Thu Apr 16, 2026
summary: Official Count: 54 Al-Shabaab Fighters Killed in Juba Regions Airstrikes; NISA Kills 9 in Separate Lower Shabelle Operation. Somalia Condemns Israel's Appointment of Ambassador to Somaliland as Direct Violation of Sovereignty.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Wed Apr 15, 2026
summary: SNA and Jubbaland Forces Launch Large-Scale Offensive; Airstrikes Hit Al-Shabaab in Juba Regions With International Support.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Tue Apr 14, 2026
summary: Somalia's 11th Federal Parliament formally expired its four-year constitutional mandate on April 14, 2026, with no elections held or agreed upon. AUSSOM Sector 1 Troops Distribute Food and Medicine to Mubarak Residents.

summary: SNA 66th Anniversary: Turkish F-16 Flyover Mogadishu; President Mohamud Warns Jubbaland and Puntland of Federal Military Reach.

summary: Somalia President Defends Uganda's Military Role After Gen. Muhoozi Questions Turkey's Expanding Security Footprint.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: Turkey: Somalia Offshore Drilling Could Yield Oil in 6–9 Months; Parliamentary Scrutiny Over TPAO Concession Intensifies.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026
summary: Turkey's Drillship Çağrı Bey Arrives in Mogadishu for Somalia's First Offshore Oil Drilling; Opposition MP Raises Alarm Over 'Imbalanced' Deal.

title: Somalia Conflict Update — Apr 09, 2026
summary: Somalia's constitutional crisis reached its most critical countdown with the April 14 parliamentary mandate deadline five days away and no FGS-opposition agreement in sight. Al-Shabaab struck a significant blow on April 5 when SNA 14th October Brigade commander Col. Nur Farey was assassinated by a suspected infiltrator at Abdow Dibille camp near Afgooye, Lower Shabelle. AFRICOM confirmed a record 49 airstrikes in Q1 2026 — on track to far exceed all prior annual totals. OCHA's April 6 Drought Situation Report No. 5 revealed the $852M Humanitarian Response Plan is only 13.4% funded (86.6% gap), with 4.4 million Somalis in IPC Phase 3+ food insecurity; Gu rains are expected to begin in mid-April but will not reverse crisis-level conditions by May.

summary: Somalia Assumes African Union Peace and Security Council Seat for the First Time in the Body's 23-Year History.

summary: OCHA's Somalia 2025–2026 Drought Emergency Situation Report No.

title: Somalia Conflict Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: Somalia's constitutional crisis entered its most critical phase as the parliamentary mandate expiry deadline of April 14 approaches, with the opposition 'Council for the Future of Somalia' (Puntland, Jubbaland, opposition groups) demanding a negotiated electoral agreement and rejecting term extensions under the disputed March 2026 constitutional amendments. On the military front, elite Gorgor Commandos (203rd Unit, 20th Brigade) and UPDF AUSSOM troops captured senior Al-Shabaab battalion commander Salaad Cusmaan Macalin ('Sahm') at Mubarak on April 3 — revealing that a Kenyan-national associate had volunteered for a suicide mission. On April 4, NISA and partners killed 9 Al-Shabaab militants and destroyed an IED factory in Kunya Barrow and Togarey, Lower Shabelle. AFRICOM reached nearly 50 airstrikes in Q1 2026, a record-breaking pace far exceeding all prior annual totals. The WFP food aid crisis deepened critically with 6.5 million Somalis facing hunger and the humanitarian response plan 76% unfunded.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: SNA 14th October Brigade Commander Col. Nur Farey Assassinated by Suspected Al-Shabaab Infiltrator Near Afgooye.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: Somalia's National Intelligence and Security Agency (NISA), in collaboration with international partners, conducted two separate operations in the Lower Shabelle region on April 4, 2026. WFP Food Aid to 6.5 Million Somalis in Crisis as Funding Reaches Catastrophic Shortfall in April.

summary: Joint AUSSOM–SNA Gorgor Operation Captures Senior Al-Shabaab Battalion Commander at Mubarak.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Thu Apr 2, 2026
summary: AFRICOM Records 49 Airstrikes in Somalia in Q1 2026 — Record-Breaking Pace Widely Reported.

title: Somalia Conflict Update — Apr 01, 2026
summary: Somalia's federal crisis reached its most acute phase when SNA forces seized Baidoa (Southwest State capital) on March 30, forcing President Laftagareen's resignation — the first federal military takeover of a member state capital in Somalia's history. Puntland withdrew recognition of the Federal Government on March 31, leaving Somalia's governance architecture severely fractured as parliamentary mandates approach expiry (April 14). On the military front, SNA and AUSSOM repelled an Al-Shabaab assault at Mubarak (40 killed, March 26), killed 21 fighters in Bay Region's Daynuunay (March 29), and AFRICOM conducted a confirmed strike 132 km northwest of Kismayo (March 27). UNHCR confirmed 50,000+ newly displaced by Southwest State clashes, while UNICEF warned that 6.5 million Somalis face acute hunger with the humanitarian funding gap widening to ~76% of the $852M requirement.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: Somali National Army forces declared Baidoa fully secured on March 31, with remaining Southwest State loyalist security forces confined to scattered pockets. Puntland Withdraws Recognition of Federal Government, Declares Independent Operation.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: SNA Forces Seize Baidoa — Southwest State Capital Falls; Laftagareen Resigns. UNHCR: 50,000+ Newly Displaced by Southwest State Clashes.

title: Somalia Conflict Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: Southwest State's parliament re-elected President Laftagareen by 66–13 votes on March 28, cementing a tripartite opposition bloc — Southwest State, Puntland, and Jubbaland — against Mogadishu's March 2026 constitutional amendments; on March 25, the Federal Government bypassed Southwest State by unilaterally appointing Bakool officials and the JSP Secretary General resigned accusing President Mohamud of running a 'one-man show.' UNICEF Executive Director Catherine Russell visited Dollow on March 27 and issued an emergency warning that 6.5 million Somalis face acute hunger — up 1.7 million since January — with the 2025 Deyr cereal harvest 83% below the 30-year average and Iran-Israel war supply chain disruptions threatening to raise humanitarian transit costs 30–60%. On the military front, elite Gorgor Commandos conducted operations in War-Iise district (Middle Shabelle) on March 24, while SNA and AUSSOM/UPDF forces killed 30+ al-Shabaab fighters in a joint operation at Bulunagaad Base in Lower Shabelle as Operation Rolling Thunder continued its Ramadan offensive.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Sat Mar 28, 2026
summary: Laftagareen Re-Elected Southwest State President as Constitutional Standoff Deepens. SNA and AUSSOM Kill 30+ Al-Shabaab Fighters in Bulunagaad Joint Operation.

title: Somalia Conflict Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: UNICEF: 6.5 Million Somalis Face Acute Hunger, Iran-War Supply Chains Add New Risk. US Africa Command (AFRICOM) conducted a confirmed airstrike targeting Al-Shabaab approximately 132 km northwest of Kismayo, in the Jubbaland region of southern Somalia. An EU NAVFOR Operation Atalanta vessel located a hijacked dhow approximately 480 nautical miles southeast of Mogadishu and confirmed its identity.

title: Somalia Conflict Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: Somalia's Federal Government launched 'Operation Rolling Thunder' on March 1 alongside Ugandan AUSSOM troops in Lower Shabelle, targeting al-Shabaab strongholds and supply routes with early territorial gains. A deepening political crisis erupted in mid-March when Somalia's parliament — boycotted by the opposition — voted to extend presidential and legislative terms from four to five years via constitutional amendment, prompting Southwest State to formally suspend relations with Mogadishu and accuse the federal government of a 'blatant invasion' involving SNA troop deployments and a civilian air-traffic blockade on Baidoa. US AFRICOM continued its intensive airstrike campaign, conducting at least 47 strikes in Somalia in 2026, including a March 19 strike 80 km northeast of Jamaame targeting al-Shabaab and multiple March strikes against ISIS-Somalia in Puntland. Al-Shabaab released a propaganda video on March 22 celebrating its October 2025 Godka Jilacow prison raid, signalling continued information operations despite battlefield pressure.

summary: JSP Secretary General Resigns, Accuses President Mohamud of Running 'One-Man Show'. Federal Government Unilaterally Appoints Bakool Governor, Bypassing Southwest State.
